channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Protesters sentenced to decades in US prison over alleged antifa ties
item.description: Civil liberties advocates have expressed alarm over the case involving a protest outside Prairieland Detention Center.

item.title: US Supreme Court says Rastafarian man shaved by prison guards can’t sue
item.description: The high court has upheld ruling that prisoners cannot sue prison staff for money damages under religious liberties law.

item.title: Israel’s deliberate targeting of Palestinian children
item.description: New UN report documents the toll of Israel's war on Gaza and its occupation of the West Bank on Palestinian children.

item.title: England held by Ghana at World Cup as pair close on last-32 passage
item.description: England and Ghana share a 0-0 draw in Group L, despite a late barrage by the English.

item.title: Donald Trump to attend World Cup final, present trophy: Infantino
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino will be 'enjoying the final' with Trump and present the trophy together on July 19.

item.title: UN: Israel committed genocide by targeting Gaza children
item.description: Israel deliberately targeted Palestinian children in Gaza, resulting in genocide.

item.title: Paraguay’s Almiron suspended for one game after red card for covering mouth
item.description: FIFA also stripped journalist Jorge Chipi Vera of World Cup credentials after he criticised Almiron's red card on air.

item.title: Trump allies reassure Israelis amid tensions on US-Iran deal
item.description: Trump allies reassure Israelis amid tensions on US-Iran deal

item.title: Massive fire rips through Indonesia sandal factory
item.description: A massive fire destroyed a sandal factory in Tangerang, Indonesia, after it spread to a gas storage area.

item.title: UN starts evacuating 11,000 stranded sailors from Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Following the start of the US-Israel war on Iran on February 28, Tehran had effectively closed off the vital waterway.

item.title: Alibaba sues US military over labelling it a ‘Chinese military company’
item.description: E-commerce giant says that claims of affiliation with Chinese military have 'no basis in fact or law'.

item.title: Trump disputes Iran’s rejection of nuclear inspectors
item.description: President Trump pushed back on Iran’s foreign ministry claim that there are no plans to allow IAEA nuclear inspectors.

item.title: India prepares contingency plans due to weak monsoon season
item.description: Monsoon rains so far have been 43 percent below average, which is expected to impact India's crops.

item.title: Rubio begins Gulf visit in the UAE to advance US-Iran peace deal
item.description: Arriving in the UAE at the start of a Gulf tour, Rubio is seeking to advance the US-Iran deal with regional partners.

item.title: Sanchez warns he ‘will not recognise’ Fujimori victory in Peru election
item.description: The left-wing candidate has called for voting results from 119 consular offices to be nullified over a procedure change.

item.title: Iran’s president arrives in Pakistan for peace talks
item.description: Iran’s president arrives in Pakistan for peace talks

item.title: US Senate votes to pass Iran war powers resolution in blow to Trump
item.description: The resolution is the first to pass both the Senate and the House, though it is likely to face a presidential veto.

item.title: US says Iran can stay for two days before Egypt World Cup game
item.description: Iran's team will still need to leave the US shortly after their third group-stage match against Egypt on Friday.

item.title: Ronaldo scores twice as Portugal thrash Uzbekistan 5-0 at World Cup 2026
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes first player to score at six World Cup tournaments as Portugal thrash debutants Uzbekistan.

item.title: Displaced Lebanese return south under fragile ceasefire deal
item.description: Displaced Lebanese are heading back south after a US-Iran backed ceasefire halted months of Israeli bombardment.

item.title: Video: Israeli troops seen patrolling Syrian countryside
item.description: Israeli troops and two Merkava tanks have been spotted in the Syrian countryside near Quneitra city.

item.title: US Supreme Court OKs ExxonMobil lawsuit over Castro-era property seizure
item.description: The US Supreme Court made it easier for US companies to seek compensation from Cuba's government for assets seized.

item.title: US Supreme Court backs Trump policy on green card holder rights
item.description: Court rules 6-3 that suspicion alone justifies placing green card holders on immigration parole at border crossings.

item.title: Who’s being left out of the World Cup?
item.description: Can football’s biggest stage stay global as visa denials and border restrictions mount?

item.title: Why do the UK’s prime ministers keep resigning?
item.description: Keir Starmer is the latest to step down, after less than two years in office.
